{{Quote|We shall get him, Captain, not you.|Altin Wuho to [[Dirk Balor]]|<ref name="The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 149">{{StoryCite|story=Death Masque|book=The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 149}}</ref>}}
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human replica droid]], '''Altin Wuho''' was assigned to a task force led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Dirk Balor]].

==Biography==
Wuho was created for the sole purpose of detaining a [[nightmare demon]] named [[Reist]]. Nightmare demons were able to influence the thought of organics, causing their worst nightmares to come true and driving the victim into insanity. They made the perfect weapon, but because organics were unable to control them due to their powers, a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was needed to control the beast.

Wuho kept the creature under lock and key. Reist was detained in a [[Force field/Legends|force field]] and hooded with a [[Lead/Legends|lead]] cap. This kept Reist from affecting any of Dirk Balor's crew. Wuho would place the creature in a hollowed out missile tube and then remove his cap. The tube would then be remotely flown to land on a [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Reist's powers would drive the crew to insanity, and the Imperials would claim their victims without a shot being fired.

Wuho eventually became incredibly arrogant, realizing the importance of his position. He would talk back to the captain and insult him for being so frightened of Reist. One fateful day, Balor's task force [[Skirmish on Lapez 3|intercepted]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in the [[Lapez system/Legends|Lapez system]]. Wuho placed Reist in his missile tube and landed him right on the wing of Skywalker's [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]. The nightmare demon's power immediately began to affect Luke and he crashed his ship on [[Lapez 3]].

Wuho was proud of his pet's accomplishment, and Balor ordered Wuho to the surface to retrieve Reist and Skywalker's remains. However, when Wuho arrived he was astounded to find Reist dead. In disbelief, Wuho tried to question Skywalker on how he had defeated the beast's power. Skywalker was in no mood to talk and he pulled a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on the droid. Wuho's droid reflexes were more than a match for the young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], and he quickly shot the blaster out of Skywalker's hands.
[[File:WuhoDeath.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Altin Wuho is killed by Luke Skywalker.]]
Skywalker had another trick up his sleeve, and he used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to propel his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] straight into Wuho's chest, destroying him. Skywalker retrieved the stabilization unit housed within Wuho's droid body to repair his X-wing, which was damaged in the crash. Skywalker left the destroyed droid's body on the planet.
